CHAPTER 188
(H. B. 161)
AN ACT to prohibit the taking, enticing, assisting to escape, or
the encouraging to escape of lawfully committed inmates from
the State Hospitals for the Insane and from the Feeble-Minded
Institute; declaring such taking, assisting, enticing, or en
couraging a misdemeanor; providing the punishment therefor.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Common-wealth of Kentucky:
§ 1. Any person who shall take a lawfully com
mitted inmate from a State Hospital for the Insane
or from the Feeble-Minded Institute without the
consent of the superintendent of any such institu
tion or who shall entice, assist, or encourage any
such inmate to escape from any such state institution
shall be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor and upon
conviction in the circuit court in the county in which
any such escape was affected shall be punished by
confinement in the County Jail for a period not to
exceed six (6) months.
All laws or parts of laws that conflict herewith are
hereby repealed. Approved February 26, 1940.

classification.title: An Act to amend Chapter 1 of the First Special Session of the 1938 General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, relating to the retirement system for teachers.
classification.summary: This act amends existing statutes concerning the teachers' retirement system in Kentucky. It adds new sections and subsections, and modifies existing ones to provide for the administration and management of the retirement system and its funds by a Board of Trustees selected under the supervision of the Superintendent of Public Instruction through a vote of the system's members. The definition of 'Teacher' is also expanded to include staff from Eastern and Western Kentucky State Teachers Colleges.

classification.title: An Act relating to the State Teachers Retirement System

It specifies eligibility for membership in the retirement system, including employees of various state educational institutions, the Superintendent of Public Instruction, division heads, and professional staff of the State Department of Education, as well as members of local retirement systems who join the state system. It also defines 'Prior Service' as years a member was a 'Teacher' in Kentucky before July 1, 1941, with a maximum of thirty years, and 'Subsequent Service' as years after July 1, 1941, while a member of the state retirement system.

classification.title: An Act relating to the Teacher Retirement System
classification.summary: This act details the election process for the Board of Trustees of the Teacher Retirement System, including the nomination committee composition and voting rights for teachers. It specifies that the election for the Board of Trustees in 1940 shall be held by June 1st and conducted by ballot under the supervision of the Superintendent of Public Instruction. Teachers in schools covered by the Act are deemed members of the retirement system and have voting rights. The nominating committee includes delegates from the Kentucky Education Association and representatives from the Kentucky Negro Education Association. Subsequent elections will follow the same nomination procedures. All election expenses will be paid by the Board of Trustees.
classification.reasoning: The statute explicitly mentions the 'Kentucky Negro Education Association' and its president and a member appointed by him. This indicates a racial component in the structure of the nominating committee for the Teacher Retirement System's Board of Trustees. While the statute itself doesn't mandate segregation or discrimination, the explicit inclusion of a separate association for Black educators in the nomination process suggests a system that acknowledges and potentially perpetuates racial distinctions within the educational system. This warrants further human review to determine if this structure was part of a broader Jim Crow framework or a mechanism for ensuring representation within a segregated system. The confidence is moderate because the statute itself doesn't directly enact discriminatory practices but rather describes a process that involves racially distinct organizations.

CHAPTER 193 797
proceeding so removed to the Commission shall be
heard and determined by the entire Commission
composed of three members, except that no member
of the Commission shall participate in any case in
which he is an interested party, and the Chairman
shall act alone in the absence or disqualification of
any other member. The Commission shall promptly
notify the parties to any proceedings of its findings
and decisions.
(g) Procedure. The manner in which appealed
claims shall be presented and the conduct of hear
ings and appeals shall be in accordance with regu
lations prescribed by the Commission for deter
mining the rights of the parties, such hearings to be
conducted in a summary manner. A full and com
plete record shall be kept of all proceedings in con
nection with an appealed claim. All testimony at
any hearing upon an appealed claim shall be re
corded, but need not be transcribed unless the claim
is further appealed. No examiner, referee or mem
ber of the Commission shall participate in any
hearing in which he is an interested party.
(h) Witness Fees. Witnesses subposnaed pur
suant to proceedings under this Section shall be
allowed fees in accordance with rates prescribed in
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes. Such fees and all
expenses of proceedings involving disputed claims
shall be deemed a part of the expense of administer
ing this Act.
'(i) Appeals to Courts. Any decision of the
Commission, in the absence of an appeal therefrom
as herein provided, shall become final twenty days
after the date thereof; and judicial review thereof
shall be permitted only after any party claiming to
be aggrieved thereby has exhausted his remedies
before the Commission, as provided by this Act.

mission may enter into administrative arrangements

ernment similarly charged with the administration

4748g-14 — Unemployment Insurance Fund.
(a) Establishment and Control. There is here
by established a special fund to be known as the
Unemployment Insurance Fund which shall be ad
ministered separate and apart from all public
moneys or funds of the State. This fund shall con
sist of :
(1) All contributions and moneys collected under
this Act, together with any penalties thereon col
lected pursuant to Section 8 of this Act ;
(2) All fines and penalties collected pursuant to
the provisions of this Act;
(3) Interest earned upon any moneys in the
fund;
(4) Any property or securities acquired through
the use of moneys belonging to the fund; and
(5) All earnings of such property or securities.
Subject to the provisions of this Act, the Com
mission is vested hereby with full power, authority,
and jurisdiction over the fund, including all moneys
and property or securities belonging thereto, and
may perform any and all acts, whether or not herein
specifically designated, which are necessary or con
venient in the administration thereof consistent with
the provisions of this Act.

(f ) Emergency Diversion to the Pooled Account.
If, on January 1 or July 1 of any year, the amount

highest total amount of benefits paid from such
account during any one of the preceding three calen
dar years, the Commission shall have power to
declare, with respect to the six months beginning on

During such period, one per cent of the wages upon

shall be paid into the pooled account from such con
tributions. During such period the force and effect
of any section of this Act shall be suspended if such
section provides that any subject employer may pay
contributions at a rate of less than one (1) per cent,
and every subject employer shall pay contributions

contributions are to be measured in accordance with

DECLARATION OF EMERGENCY
Whereas, the foregoing changes in the Kentucky
Unemployment Compensation Law now codified as
Sections 4748 g-1 to 4748 g-22, inclusive, Kentucky
Statutes, Baldwin's 1938 Supplement, are necessary
in order to (1) bring said act into conformity with
certain amendments to the Social Security Act, (2)
make possible a more effective administration, and
(3) remove existing inequities; and whereas, the
attainment of such purposes demands immediate
action by this body, an emergency is hereby declared
to exist and this Act shall become effective on
April 1, 1940. Approved March 15, 1940.

CHAPTER 198
(S. B. 192)
AN ACT amending Section 4893, Kentucky Statutes. Baldwin's
Kentucky Statutes Service, Supplementing Carroll's Statute
and Code, 1939 Edition, and Section 4897, Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes, Baldwin's 1936 Revision, both relating to workmen's
compensation.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
That Section 4893, Kentucky Statutes, Baldwin's
Kentucky Statutes Service, 1939 Edition, Supple
ment to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes, Baldwin's
1936 Revision, and Section 4897, Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes, Baldwin's 1936 Edition, be and the same
are hereby amended by repealing said sections, and
each of them in their entirety and reenacting- said
sections so that as amended and reenacted they will
read as follows to-wit :
"Section 4893. To whom compensation is pay
able in case of death ; burial expenses ; no depend
ents; where dependents; partial dependents. —If
death results within two years from an accident for
which compensation is payable under this act, the
employer or his insurer shall pay to the person
entitled to compensation, or, if none, then to the
personal representative of the deceased employee
reasonable burial expenses of a person of the stand
ard of living of the deceased, not to exceed the sum
of $150.00, and shall also pay to or for the following
persons compensation as follows, to-wit:
(1) If there are no dependents, as herein
defined, there shall be paid, in addition to the burial
expenses and medical expenses, if any, otherwise
provided for herein, the further sum of $100, pay
ment to be made to the personal representative of
the deceased employee.
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1940, p. 867

seven days thereof, shall pay the employee so injured

dred dollars ($7,500.00). In case the period of total

shall be deducted from the total period of ten years

such partial disability shall be deducted from the

($7,500.00). In case of the following injuries the
disability shall be deemed total and permanent,
to -wit :
1. The total permanent loss of sight in both
eyes.
2. The loss of both feet at or above the ankle.
3. The loss of both hands at or above the wrist.
4. A similar loss of one hand and one foot.
5. An injury to the spine, resulting in permanent
and complete paralysis of both arms or both legs,
and of one arm and one leg.
6. An injury to the skull resulting in incurable
insanity or imbecility.
The above enumeration is not to be taken as
exclusive, but in all other cases the burden of proof
shall be on the claimant to prove that his injuries
have resulted in permanent total disability."
Approved by the Governor March 20, 1940.

WHEREAS, the General Assembly deems the compensation of

Representatives, as provided for in Chapter 88 of the Acts of

tioning of the business of the Senate during the 1940 regular
session; now, therefore,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
§ 1. That there is hereby appropriated from the
Legislative Session Fund as additional compensa
tion for the following officers and employees of the
Senate and the House of Representatives : Assist
ant Clerk, Enrolling Clerk, Sergeant at Arms, Door
keeper, Cloakroom Keeper, Janitor and Pages, to
that compensation as provided for in Chapter 88 of
the Acts of the General Assembly of 1936, the sum
of Thirty Dollars ($30.00) per diem, for each body,
for the duration of the 1940 regular Session.
§ 2. That the aforesaid amount shall be pro
rated among the aforenamed constitutional officers
and employees, according to the per diem set forth
in Chapter 88 of the Acts of the General Assembly
of 1936, and shall be by the Department of Finance
charged to the account of the aforenamed officers
and employees of the Senate and the House of
Representatives on this basis.
§ 3. That the Clerk of the Senate and the Clerk
of the House of Representatives are directed to
issue vouchers to the Commissioner of Finance, who
shall draw his warrant in favor of the Clerk of the
Senate and the Clerk of the House of Repre
sentatives for the above mentioned amounts, and

WHEREAS, C. L. Alderson, of Jefferson County, Kentucky was

WHEREAS, the State Live Stock Sanitary Board awarded in
demnity to the said C. L. Alderson to be paid by the Common

WHEREAS, the said C. L. Alderson has not been paid the sum
awarded to him as indemnity because the funds appropriated
for such purposes by the General Assembly for the fiscal year
ending June 30, 1940, are insufficient and sums appropriated
for subsequent fiscal years could not be lawfully expended for
the purpose of reimbursing the said C. L. Alderson; and
WHEREAS, the State Veterinarian has approved the payment
of the hereinabove mentioned amount to replace the cattle so
destroyed, now, therefore,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
The Department of Finance is hereby directed to
draw a warrant on the Treasurer payable to C. L.
Alderson to be payable from the appropriation for
the fiscal year 1939-40 to the State Live Stock
Sanitary Board for the payment of indemnities for
cattle destroyed by order of the board in the sum of
Eight Hundred and Thirty-five Dollars and
Seventy-eight cents or for whatever amount there
may be left in said fund in the event there is not
sufficient money in the fund to pay the full amount.
Neither approved nor disapproved.

Treasury the sum of fifty-nine dollars and forty cents in pay
ment of a tax which was not lawfully due, and no remedy
exists to enable him to secure a refund, now, therefore,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
That there is appropriated to F. M. Tapp, of
Dixon, Kentucky, out of the General Fund, the sum
of fifty-nine dollars and forty cents; the Depart
ment of Finance is authorized and directed to draw
its warrant on the State Treasurer for that sum,
and upon presentation of the warrant to him the
State Treasurer shall issue his check to the said
F. M. Tapp in payment of said sum.
Neither approved nor disapproved.

850 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 219
(S. R. 61)
A JOINT RESOLUTION authorizing Lee Higginson to sue the
Commonwealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Com
mission.
WHEREAS, about the year 1929 the State Highway Department
of Kentucky constructed a bridge in Webster County, Kentucky
on U. S. Highway No. 41, which bridge is between Dixon, Ken
tucky and Providence, Kentucky, over what was formerly
known as Bull Creek, and that the farm of Lee Higginson is
on the south side of said road and bridge; that his said lands
are level bottom lands. That said bridge is so constructed that
it casts the water upon and over the lands of said Higginson,
which has damaged his lands and crops growing thereon.
In the year 1931 the State Highway Department entered into
in agreement with the said Lee Higginson to clean out the silt
and debris from under the bridge and to riprap the ditch in
such way as to confine the water to the ditch for such a dis
tance that the water would assume a straight course running
with the ditch. Pursuant to said agreement, the State High
way Department did make some attempt to carry out said
agreement but it did not complete the work in such manner
as to confine the water to the ditch and it strikes the bank of
the ditch on the west side at a right angle and now casts the
water over the lands of said Lee Higginson and has since said
work was completed in the year 1932. Since that time the
water has continued to flow over his said lands and to drown
his crops; and he has been greatly and materially damaged by
reason thereof, all of which statements are supported by affi
davits attached hereto.
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
That the said Lee Higginson be and he is hereby
authorized and permitted to sue the Common
wealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Com
mission in the Webster Circuit Court of Webster
County for such damages as he may have sustained
by reason of the manner in which the bridge herein
mentioned is constructed and maintained and the
riprap along said ditch has been maintained.
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1940, p. 897

The bridge's construction allegedly caused water to be cast upon Higginson's lands, damaging his property and crops. An agreement was made in 1931 for the State Highway Department to address the issue, but the work was not completed satisfactorily, leading to ongoing damage.

water over the lands of said Childers and has since said work
was completed in the year 1932. Since that time the water
has continued to flow over his said lands and to drown his
crops; and he has been greatly and materially damaged by
reason thereof, all of which statements are supported by affi
davits attached hereto.
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
That the said Shirley Childers be and he is hereby
authorized and permitted to sue the Commonwealth
of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission
in the Webster Circuit Court of Webster County for
such damages as he may have sustained by reason
of the manner in which the bridge herein mentioned
is constructed and maintained and the riprap along
said ditch has been maintained.
No plea of limitation shall be imposed.
The Attorney General may investigate and com
promise said claim if he deems it advisable.
if recovery is had by suit or compromise, the
amount so adjudged or compromised shall be paid
by the Commissioner of Finance by warrant drawn
upon the State Treasurer and paid out of the
General Fund.
Either party to said suit may appeal from any
judgment which may be rendered, to the Court of
Appeals of Kentucky.

WHEREAS, Frank Ahmann was a member of the State Police

year 1935, and
WHEREAS, on April 5, 1935, he received a written order from
Jess Stone, his superior officer in charge of said State Police,
which directed him to escort the funeral of the late Wood
Anxton from Louisville to Owensboro on April 6th, and
WHEREAS, in complying with this order he did go to Owens
boro and returned to Louisville and while en route to his home
he was struck by an automobile at 24th and Market Street and
his left leg was broken in three places, and
WHEREAS, he was confined three weeks in St. Marys and
Elizabeth Hospital in Louisville, and
WHEREAS, for seven months thereafter he was forced to keep
his leg in a plaster cast, and
WHEREAS, he incurred in the necessary treatment of this
injury hospital bills, ambulance bills and doctor bills in large
sums for the payment of which no provision was made or has
yet been made, and
WHEREAS, these injuries were received while he was in the
line of duty,
Be it Resolved by the General. Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:

856 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 223
(S. R. 67)
A JOINT RESOLUTION authorizing Mrs. Fannie Daniel to sue
the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Com
mission or either of them.
WHEREAS, on the 6th day of October, 1932, F. Daniel of Paints-
ville, Kentucky, while traveling upon the Mayo Trail (U. S.
Highway No. 23) had the automobile belonging to his wife,
Mrs. Fannie Daniel, which he was driving, damaged by col
lision with a large truck parked on the curve without any
lights or the giving of any warning or signal of any kind to
warn the public of any danger when approaching said truck,
said truck being the property of the State of Kentucky and
being operated and used at the time by the employees of the
State Highway Commission in the performance of their duties,
and
WHEREAS, said collision was caused by reason of the alleged
carelessness and negligence of the employees of the State
Highway Commission, in the conduct of the State's business,
now, therefore,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
§ 1. That Mrs. Fannie Daniel or her heirs or
personal representatives, if she be deceased, be,
and they are hereby, authorized and permitted to
sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the State
Highway Commission, or either of them, in the
Circuit Court of the County of her residence for
such damages as she may have suffered, if any, by
reason of any injury to her automobile through the
carelessness or negligence of the State Highway
Commission, its agents, or employees. In the event
any judgment is recovered by said Mrs. Fannie
Daniel in said suit for injuries to her automobile
or same is settled or compromised, same shall be
paid by the Commissioner of Finance by warrant
drawn on the State Treasurer, paid out of the
General Fund.

(S. R. 69)
A RESOLUTION for the benefit of the employees of the House
and Senate.
WHEREAS, the present employees and officers of the General
Assembly have rendered faithful service during all sessions of
the General Assembly, meeting in regular session, during the
year 1940, and
WHEREAS, the General Assembly, in view of the services ren
dered, deems the compensation allowed by law as provided for
In Chapter 88 of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1936,
Inadequate, now, therefore,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
§ 1. That there is hereby appropriated from the
Legislative Session Fund as additional compensa
tion, for the following employees and officers of
the Senate and the House of Representatives, viz.:
Assistant Clerks, Enrolling Clerks, Sergeant at
Arms, Doorkeeper, Cloakroom Keepers, Janitors,
and Pages, to that compensation provided in Chapter
88 of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1936, the
sum of Nine Thousand, Nine Hundred and Nineteen
($9,919.00) Dollars.
§ 2. That the Chief Clerk of the Senate shall
requisition and the Commissioner of Finance shall
draw a warrant for the sum of Four Thousand Nine
Hundred Fifty-five ($4,955.00) Dollars on the State
Treasurer of the Commonwealth of Kentucky in
favor of the Chief Clerk of the Senate. Such Chief
Clerk shall forthwith distribute to the employees
and officers of the Senate such funds on the basis
of the number of days that such employees and
officers served during the regular sessions of the
General Assembly in the year 1940, the rate per
day to be computed by said Chief Clerk and the
presiding officer of the Senate on a pro rata basis

CHAPTER 226 861
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
§ 1. There is hereby appropriated $1,100.00 out
of the General Expenditure Fund to be used by the
State Librarian for the purchase of the Kentucky
Directory, 1940 Edition.
§ 2. That the State Librarian shall purchase,
at not exceeding two dollars per copy, five hundred
and fifty copies of the book entitled "Kentucky
Directory 1940," for the use of the Courts, state and
county officers and members of the General AS
sembly. The Librarian shall furnish the members
of the General Assembly and such officers as are
designated by the presiding officers of the respective
houses a copy of same and shall furnish the state
officials with copies thereof and shall send a copy
to the circuit court clerk of each county in the state
to be kept in his office. The Librarian shall furnish
the Librarian of each state and territory of the Union
a copy of said books, and a copy to the Librarian of
Congress, and shall deliver to the Secretary of State
fifty copies to be exchanged with the Secretaries of
State of other states for directories of their state.
Upon receipt of the books the Librarian shall
certify the bill therefor to the Commissioner of
Finance and he shall draw his warrant on the State
Treasurer for the amount certified which shall be
paid out of the General Expenditure Fund.
§ 3. It being necessary that the officers should
have the books herein provided for at once, an
emergency is declared and this resolution shall take
effect upon its adoption and approval by the
Governor.
Neither approved nor disapproved.

CHAPTER 227
(H. R. 7)
A RESOLUTION authorizing Malcolm Godsey, Jr., by his statu
tory guardian, Malcom Godsey, Sr., to sue the Commonwealth
of Kentucky, and the State Highway Commission of Kentucky,
or either.
Said resolution is as follows, viz.:
WHEREAS, on April 13, 1939, Malcolm Godsey, Jr., an infant,
7 years of age, was walking alongside State Highway No. 15,
near Duane, in Perry County, Kentucky, and was hit and In
jured by State Highway Maintenance Truck No. 2189, at the
time being driven by one Grit Bach, resulting in the breaking
and complete fracture of the femur bone of said infant's leg,
and the cutting of his head, body, arms, legs and person in
divers places, and said infant was confined to the Hazard Hos
pital and his home for about six months as a result of said
injuries, and now has a crooked foot and has sustained per
manent and lasting disability as a direct result of said injuries;
and
WHEREAS, it is claimed by said Godsey that the driver of said
truck operated the same in a reckless and negligent manner,
striking said infant at a time when he was off the highway,
and that said infant was free of any fault, and
WHEREAS, it is claimed by the said Malcolm Godsey, Sr., guard
ian of Malcolm Godsey, Jr., that said infant suffered great
physical and mental pain and agony, and his power to earn
money was greatly diminished and damaged as a direct result
of said injury.
Now, therefore in order to determine by judicial action, the
liability of the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the State
Highway Commission of Kentucky for the alleged damages and
injury: —
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
§ 1. That Malcolm Godsey, Jr., by his statutory
guardian, Malcolm Godsey, Sr., be and he is hereby
authorized and permitted to sue the Commonwealth
of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission
of Kentucky, or either, in the Perry Circuit Court,
for such damages, if any, as he may have suffered,

WHEREAS, W. B. Lackey, Chief of Police, of Richmond, Ken
tucky, was notified after the commission of the crime that said

mediately, thereafter, the said W. B. Lackey went to St. Louis,

Lackey presented a claim to the Commonwealth of Kentucky

WHEREAS, said claim was rejected because the said J. B. Wunch

Making arrests for felony, between October 24, 1934 and
October 11, 1935;
Summoning witnesses for the Commonwealth in felony cases,
between October 24, 1934 and October 11, 1935;
Conveying Ruley Henson from Ashland, Kentucky, to Sandy
Hook, Kentucky, a distance of seventy-seven miles, on October
11, 1935, and conveying Charlie Crum from Morehead, Ken
tucky, to Sandy Hook, Kentucky, a distance of thirty miles, on
March 23, 1935; and
Waiting on the Special December Term, 1934, of the Elliott
Circuit Court, three days; on the Regular March, 1935, Term,
six days; on the Regular July, 1935, Term, six days; and on
the Regular October. 1935, Term, five days; and
WHEREAS, the claim has been rejected by the State of Ken
tucky, and more than ninety days have elapsed since the end
of the fiscal year ending June 30, 1935 : Now, therefore,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:

WHEREAS, on or about the 12th day of August, 1939, on a public
highway commonly known as Left Beaver Creek Road, Bailey
Mosely, Floyd County, suffered serious and permanent personal
injuries, and the car which he owned and was operating at
that time was completely demolished, and
WHEREAS, said personal injuries and property damage was
brought about by the alleged gross negligence and carelessness
of one Ralph Allen, employee of the Department of Highways
and/or the Commonwealth of Kentucky while in the conduct of
the business of the Commonwealth of Kentucky: therefore
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
§ 1. Bailey Mosely be and is hereby authorized
and permitted to file suit against the Commonwealth
of Kentucky and the Department of Highways, or
either, for the purpose of determining the liability
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the Depart
ment of Highways, or either, for such damage,
personal or property, if any there be, arising from
such injuries as he may have suffered, if any, or
such destruction of his said automobile, by reason
of the carelessness or negligence of the Common
wealth of Kentucky, its agents or employees, and
in the event any judgment is recovered by the said
Bailey Mosely in said suit for injuries to himself
or his property, or same is compromised or settled,
same shall be paid by the Commissioner of Finance
by warrant drawn on the State Treasurer and paid
out of the general fund.
If any suit is instituted under this resolution, the
amount of recovery shall be limited to one thousand
dollars ($1,000.00).
Neither approved nor disapproved.

CHAPTER 252
(H. R. 63)
RESOLUTION authorizing S. H. Etherly to sue the Common
wealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission.
WHEREAS, on the 12th day of August, 1937 at or about the hour
of 9:45 A.M., about two miles northwest of Shelby ville. Ken
tucky, on the public highway between Shelbyville and Louis
ville, Kentucky, in a collision between a truck owned by the
State of Kentucky, then being operated, managed and con
trolled and used by an employee of the State Highway Com
mission of the Commonwealth of Kentucky in the performance
of his duties on the State Highway between Louisville and
Shelbyville, and an automobile owned and operated by S. H.
Etherly, the said S. H. Etherly was injured, and
WHEREAS, S. H. Etherly sustained serious and permanent per
sonal injuries and property damage by reason of the alleged
carelessness and negligence of the employees of the State High
way Commission in the conduct of the business of the State
of Kentucky.
NOW, in order that S. H. Etherly may have his "Day in Court"
to determine by judicial action the question of negligence, if
any, of the employees of the State Highway Commission of the
State of Kentucky, causing the injuries and damages aforesaid.
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
That, S. H. Etherly, in his own right and name,
be and he is hereby empowered and authorized to
file and prosecute appropriate actions against the
Commonwealth of Kentucky and the State Highway
Commission, or either, for the purpose of de
termining the liability of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky, and the State Highway Commission, or
either, for such injuries and damages, if any there
be; and in the event any judgment in either action
is recovered by said S. H. Etherly, or same are
compromised or settled, same shall be paid by the
Commissioner of Finance by warrants drawn on
the State Treasurer and paid out of the general
fund.

torical event; therefore,

906 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
WHEREAS, it la alleged that, on or about February 17, 1939,
Harry D. Nance of Graves County, Kentucky was operating a
motor truck, of which he was the owner, upon State Highway
No. 121 in the village of Farmington, Graves County, Kentucky;
and,
WHEREAS, it is alleged that at said time and place a motor
truck owned by the Commonwealth of Kentucky or by the
Department of Highways, and at that time engaged in its serv
ice, was also being operated upon said highway by an employee
of the Department of Highways in and about the business of
the Department of Highways, and was so carelessly and negli
gently operated as to run over, against and upon the motor
truck of Harry D. Nance; and,
WHEREAS, it is alleged that, as a direct and proximate result
of the negligence and carelessness of the operator of said high
way truck, Harry D. Nance received severe wounds and in
juries to his person and was, and is, permanently injured and
his power to earn money has been permanently reduced, and
he incurred considerable expense incident to the treatment of
injuries and his said motor truck was injured and damaged;
now therefore:
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
§ 1. Harry D. Nance is hereby authorized to sue
the Commonwealth of Kentucky or the Department
of Highways, or both for such sum as will fairly
and reasonably compensate him for the injuries and
damages, if any, sustained by him as a direct and
proximate result of the negligence, if any, of the
operator of said State Highway truck.
§ 2. The action herein authorized may be
brought in the Graves Circuit Court, same being
the Circuit Court having jurisdiction in Graves
County, the county wherein Harry D. Nance resides
and in which the injuries and damages were in
curred. Suit shall be commenced within one year
from the effective date of this Resolution, and no
plea of limitation shall be interposed if suit is com
menced within that time.

dren lost their father as a result of injuries sustained by him

man Moore, Therefore,

and customers visiting said cafeteria or restaurant and did
permit the floor to become covered with milk, which caused
the floor to become in an unsafe condition and did permit the
said floor to remain in said condition for some period of time,
and,
WHEREAS, the said Mona Pearl Jones Kidd, an infant under the
age of twenty-one, while in the course of the performance of
her duties and without negligence on her part and without

of the accumulation of milk thereon, and as a result thereof
she was caused to and did sustain a severe injury to her hand
so that it was necessary for her to obtain and she did obtain
surgical treatment requiring an operation, and that as a result

CHAPTER 267 919
AND WHEREAS, the Attorney General and his assistants were
unable to act as attorneys for the said Gordon Ruffner who was
thereafter indicted, tried on October 6, 1939, and acquitted in
the Pulaski Circuit Court, and by reason of said emergency
existing Honorable A. B. Chandler, then Governor of Kentucky,
employed the said B. J. Bethurum and Ben V. Smith & Son as
attorneys in order to properly protect the interest of the Com
monwealth and defend the said Gordon Ruffner but by over
sight no written request for said employment was filed in the
office of the Secretary of State, no contract in writing was
prepared or executed and no statement of the Attorney Gen
eral was filed as provided by Carroll's Kentucky Statutes, Sec
tion 112, Subsection 5,
AND WHEREAS, the said Honorable A. B. Chandler, Governor
of Kentucky, intended to pay the said attorneys out of his
emergency fund but by reason of his resignation as Governor
and appointment to the Senate of the United States prior to
the rendering of said bill for said services the said emergency
fund was not available for said payment and as a result the
said attorneys have not been paid for their services or for the
money advanced by them in the preparation of the trial which
amounts to Four Hundred Dollars for the services rendered by
the said attorneys and nineteen dollars and seventy-two cents
for expenses advanced by them in the said preparation of said
case for trial;
AND WHEREAS, said amount was not paid to the said attorneys
for the technical reasons hereinbefore state, now therefore,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
(1) That the Commission of Finance is hereby
directed to draw his warrant on the State Treasurer
of Kentucky in favor of the said B. J. Bethurum
and Ben V. Smith & Son for the sum of Four Hun
dred Nineteen Dollars and Seventy-Two Cents and
that said sum be paid to said attorneys for said
services rendered and this resolution shall take
effect immediately upon its adoption and approval
by the Governor.
Approved March 22, 1940.

CHAPTER 268
(H. R. 89)
HOUSE RESOLUTION authorizing T. E. Kendrick to sue the
Commonwealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Commis
sion.
WHEREAS, it is alleged in the summer of the year one thousand
nine hundred thirty-seven (1937), the State Highway Commis
sion of the State of Kentucky employed T. E. Kendrick, of
Monticello, Kentucky, to use a team of mules belonging to
him in the operation of a stone quarry located near said Monti-
cello then being worked by said State Highway Commission;
and whereas it is alleged that one of said mules while so
engaged in said work was instantly killed by the premature
explosion of dynamite made in connection with the operation
of said Quarry, of which the driver of said mules had no notice
or warning; now, therefore,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
§ 1. That the said T. E. Kendrick be, and he is
hereby, authorized and permitted to sue the Com
monwealth of Kentucky and the State Highway
Commission in the circuit court of Wayne County,
Kentucky, for such damage as he may have sustained
by reason of the death of said mule, if it is shown
that the death was brought about by reason of
carelessness or negligence on the part of the De
partment of Highways or its employees.
§ 2. That no plea of limitation shall be inter
posed.
§ 3. That the attorney general may investigate
and compromise said claim if he deems it advisable
so to do.
§ 4. That if recovery be had either by suit or
compromise, the amount so adjudged or com
promised shall be paid by the Commissioner of
Finance by warrant drawn on the State Treasurer
and paid out of the general fund.

A RESOLUTION authorizing Hugh Beck to sue the State High
way Commission or the Commonwealth of Kentucky or either,
for his personal injury and property damage, whereas on Jan
uary 23, 1940, in Muhlenberg County, Kentucky, it is alleged
the State Highway Department, through and by its authorized
officers, agents and servants operated a motor vehicle over and
along Federal Highway No. 62 at a point near Rockport, in
Muhlenberg County, Kentucky, in a negligent manner, and by
reason thereof collided with an ran over, into and upon a motor
vehicle then and at that time and place being operated on said
highway by Hugh Beck, and whereas it Is further alleged
Hugh Beck was injured about his person by reason of said col
lision by receiving broken bones, bruises and cuts on his body,
face and arms, which produces a permanent injury and dis
ability, and which by reason of said negligent operation of said
motor vehicle by the highway department, its agents and
servants the automobile owned by Hugh Beck was wrecked
and demolished, all of which was to his damage.
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
That Hugh Bock be and he is hereby authorized
and permitted to sue the State Highway Commis
sion or the Commonwealth of Kentucky or both in
the Circuit Court of Muhlenberg County, Kentucky,
for such damages as he may have suffered, if any,
by reason of personal injury, broken bones, medical
expense, doctors' and nurses' care, and any dam
age suffered to his property, if any, through the
carelessness and negligence of the State Highway
Commission, its agents or employees in the opera
tion of a motor vehicle into, over and upon the
motor vehicle of Hugh Beck on a state highway.
In the event this suit is compromised or settled
or in the event of a judgment, the Commissioner of
Finance shall draw his warrant in favor of Hugh
Beck on the State Treasurer, and he shall be paid
out of the general fund.

CHAPTER 275.
(H. R. 113)
A RESOLUTION authorizing the appropriation of $369.84 out of
the general fund to the Kentucky-Tennessee Light & Power
Company of Hopkinsville, Kentucky, for services rendered the
Jefferson Davis Memorial at Fairview, Kentucky.
WHEREAS, it appears that the Kentucky-Tennessee Light &
Power Company, duly authorized and qualified to sell electric
light and power in the Commonwealth of Kentucky, did from
September, 1935, to June, 1936, inclusive, provide electric cur
rent to the Jefferson Davis Memorial at Fairview, Kentucky,
and
WHEREAS, the said claims were not paid on July 1, 1936, be
cause said claims were not filed with the proper department at
that time and there were insufficient funds in the appropria
tion to the proper department to pay such claim, and
WHEREAS, the claim is for services rendered and constitutes a
just obligation of the Commonwealth of Kentucky in the
amount of $369.84, Now, Therefore,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
§ 1. There is hereby appropriated out of the
general fund the sum of $369.84 for the purpose of
paying the claim due the Kentucky-Tennessee Light
& Power Company of Hopkinsville, Kentucky.
§ 2. The said claim shall be paid out of the ap
propriation herein made, and the Commissioner of
Finance is herebv directed to draw his warrant upon
the State Treasurer who shall issue a check in pay
ment for the sum above stated.
Neither approved nor disapproved.
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1940, p. 977

alleged negligence of its employees and agents, resulting in

Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky:
§ 1. That George Sharp, Shelby Williams and
Cordelia Lee McGaughey of Anderson County, Ken
tucky, be and each of them is hereby authorized and
permitted to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky
and the Department of Highways, either or both,
in the courts of Anderson County, for such damages
as they or either of them may have sustained, if any,
by reason of any negligence or carelessness of the
Department of Highways, its agents or employees,
and in the event a judgment is obtained by either of
the parties in said suit, same shall be paid by the
Commissioner of Finance by warrant on the Treas
urer payable out of the general fund. Either party
to said suits may appeal from any judgment which
may be rendered by the courts of Anderson County
to any court of competent jurisdiction as in any
other civil action.
In the event suit is tiled, the amount to be recov
ered shall be limited to $100.00 for damage to prop
erty and $300.00 to each of the persons injured.
Nothing contained in this Resolution shall be con
sidered by either the Court or jury as substantive
evidence on the merits of the alleged claim, and the
Court shall instruct the jury that the Resolution
authorizing this suit shall not be considered as an
admission by the Commonwealth of Kentucky or any
of its agencies of any liability on the part of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky or any of its agencies,
nor an admission that any verdict of any kind what-

Neither approved nor disapproved.

CHAPTER 277 933
CHAPTER 277.
(H. R. 126)
A RESOLUTION petitioning the Congress of the United States
of America to take such steps as are necessary to cause Obion
Creek to be drained as a health project from the trestle about
one-half mile south of Pryorsburg, Kentucky, on the Illinois
Central Railroad, westward through Graves and Hickman
County at a point where said creek empties into the Mississippi
River.
WHEREAS, by reason of erosion upon a scale that can not be
controlled by private enterprise Obion Creek has been filled
from the trestle on the Illinois Central Railroad Company's
right-of-way about one-half mile south of Pryorsburg, Ken
tucky, westward through Graves and Hickman County to a
point where said creek empties into the Mississippi River,
thereby causing said creek to overflow and form slashes and
ponds along the same that stand through the middle part of
the summer each year, which ponds and slashes become breed
ing places for multiplied millions of mosquitoes that spread
malaria fever over a large portion of Graves and Hickman
County, and,
WHEREAS, the foreign demand for dark fired tobacco, which is
the money crop of said counties, has deceased to such an
extent that the farmers along said creek and whose land will
be affected by the drainage thereof are unable to incur the
expense of draining said creek without practically confiscating
the land so affected, and,
WHEREAS, the existence of said slashes and ponds constitutes
a menace to all the people living in the surrounding territory
that can be reached by the flight of mosquitoes or to which
they can be brought by wind,
Therefore be it Resolved by the House of Repre
sentatives of the Commonwealth of Kentucky:
§ 1. That the Congress of the United States of
America be petitioned to take such steps as are
necessary to cause Obion Creek to be drained from
the trestle on the Illinois Central Railroad right-
of-way about one-half mile south of Pryorsburg,
Kentucky, in Graves County, westward through said
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1940, p. 980

The creek's silting has caused it to overflow, creating breeding grounds for mosquitoes that spread malaria.
